records.ety: [AS. turf; akin to D. turf peat, G. torf, OHG. zurba turf, Sw. & Icel. torf turf, peat, Dan. törv, Skr. darbha a kind of grass, a tuft of grass. √242.]

defs: That upper stratum of earth and vegetable mold which is filled with the roots of grass and other small plants, so as to adhere and form a kind of mat; sward; sod.
quotes: At his head a grass-green turf.
The Greek historian sets her in the field on a high heap of turves.

defs: Peat, especially when prepared for fuel. See Peat.
Race course; horse racing; -- preceded by the.
notes: ☞ Turf is often used adjectively, or to form compounds which are generally self-explaining; as, turf ashes, turf cutter or turf-cutter, turf pit or turf-pit, turf-built, turf-clad, turf-covered, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Turfed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Turfing.]

defs: To cover with turf or sod; as, to turf a bank, or the border of a terrace.
